channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: California Lawmakers Urge Quebec to Lift U.S. Alcohol Restrictions Amid Trade Tensions
item.description: California legislators from both political parties have signed a letter urging Quebec Premier Christine Frechette to resume U.S. wine and spirits sales despite Canada's trade war with the U.S. Rep. Mi...

item.title: Impeachment trial highlights VP Sara Duterte's potential role in alleged threats against Marcos family
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte has sparked discussions about her possible involvement in an alleged assassination threat against President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. and his...

item.title: ICPC Chairman Urges Combating Corruption and Educating Youth for Nigeria's Future
item.description: The Chairman of Nigeria's Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC), Dr Musa Adamu Aliyu, emphasized that lasting peace in Nigeria is impossible without addressing cor...

item.title: Bank of Ghana Revokes Zeepay's License for Regulatory Non-Compliance
item.description: The Bank of Ghana (BoG) has revoked the Dedicated Electronic Money Issuer (DEMI) license of Zeepay Ghana Limited, citing multiple regulatory breaches and the company’s failure to comply with central b...

item.title: Economic Policies and Political Statements

item.title: Pokémon Winds and Waves: Dynamic Turn-Based Battle System Leaks Hint at New Gameplay Mechanics
item.description: The article discusses the upcoming Pokémon games, Winds and Waves, which are expected to feature a new battle system. While turn-based battles remain, leakers suggest dynamic changes where opposing Po...

item.title: Wall Street Banks Thrive on AI Boom, But Dimon's Concerns Loom
item.description: Major Wall Street banks, including Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase, reported record-breaking financial results for the June quarter, driven by the rapid expansion of artificial intelligence. The AI b...

item.title: Canada Post discloses $30.8 million in management bonuses as financial losses and government support draw parliamentary scrutiny
item.description: Canada Post disclosed to the House of Commons Standing Committee on Government Operations and Estimates that it paid approximately $30.8 million in bonuses to management employees during 2025, despite...

item.title: Alberta businessman faces Canada-wide arrest warrant over tax evasion allegations linked to Panama Papers
item.description: Wentao Yang, an Alberta businessman accused of tax evasion tied to the Panama Papers, now faces a Canada-wide arrest warrant after failing to appear in court. The Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) alleges Y...

item.title: South African Youth Navigate Financial Challenges Through Side Hustles and Credit Management
item.description: A recent Standard Bank Youth Barometer study reveals that South African youth are adopting prudent financial strategies, balancing immediate needs with long-term goals. Young consumers are increasingl...

item.title: Tanzania's Trade Growth and Expansion Strategies Highlighted at Sabasaba Fair
item.description: During this year’s Sabasaba agricultural and trade fair, Tanzanian businesses recorded Sh3.89 billion in trade transactions, prompting President Hussein Mwinyi to announce strategic initiatives to boo...

item.title: Tom Segura and Christina P. Announce Divorce After 18 Years of Marriage
item.description: Tom Segura and Christina P., renowned comedians and podcast hosts, have announced their separation after 18 years of marriage. The split, which is described as amicable, occurred within the last few m...

item.title: Young boy and mother critically injured in Perth car crash
item.description: A serious car crash occurred in Perth’s eastern suburbs late yesterday when a young boy and his mother were struck by a vehicle while crossing Belgravia Street in Cloverdale. The incident, which happe...

item.title: Successful Rescue of Three AAU Students by Police and Security Forces in Edo
item.description: Three female students of Ambrose Alli University (AAU) were rescued after a joint operation by police, Nigerian Army, Edo State Security Corps, and local hunters. The incident began on July 12 when ar...

item.title: Documentary Explores Life and Legacy of Poet Mary Oliver
item.description: The article discusses the documentary 'Mary Oliver: Saved by the Beauty of the World,' directed by Sasha Waters, which chronicles the poet's life from her troubled childhood to her rise as a celebrate...

item.title: ASX rises on US inflation data and banking sector earnings
item.description: Australian shares are poised for growth as US inflation data and strong bank earnings drive market optimism. US consumer prices fell in June for the first time in six years, easing pressure on the Fed...

item.title: George Conway Condemns Trump's Election Fraud Claims and Mental Fitness Concerns
item.description: George Conway, a former conservative attorney turned anti-Trump activist, criticized President Donald Trump during an appearance on 'Morning Joe' for his ongoing campaign to delegitimize the 2020 elec...

item.title: West Bengal government撤回SC对最高法院取消77个穆斯林群体OBC地位裁决的上诉
item.description: The West Bengal government has withdrawn its appeal to the Supreme Court against a Calcutta High Court order that removed the Other Backward Classes (OBC) status for 77 Muslim communities. This decisi...

item.title: Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial: Legal Disputes Over Threat Allegations
